text: Half-marathon runners with high training volume (>32 km/wk) achieve faster finish times compared to those with lower volume.
Half-marathon runners whose longest endurance run exceeds 21 km achieve faster finish times.
High training volume and long endurance runs are associated with less decline in pace during a half marathon, indicating better fatigue resistance.
Marathon runners with low training volume (<40 km/wk) have significantly slower finish times.
Marathon runners with high training volume (>65 km/wk) achieve substantially faster finish times.
Marathon runners whose longest endurance run is less than 25 km have significantly slower finish times.
Higher training volume and longer endurance runs during marathon preparation are not associated with increased running-related injury risk.
